content: We can reframe Self-Refine, the task-specific unstructured iterative self-improvement framework for LLMs, as a base modular layer that can be augmented by multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research to create a hybrid system that retains Self-Refine's zero-training advantage while fixing its limitation of uniform single-perspective feedback that often gets stuck in local optima. This hybrid framework would require no extra supervised data or model training, only structured multi-perspective prompting to enable deeper, less biased self-improvement across code generation, reasoning, and open-ended writing tasks.
